single.brief_rationale: Single agent successfully delivered all required outputs: detailed top-10 error table with rich feature context, 3+ error categories (Premium Pricing Effect, Volume Amplification, Data Quality Issues), and category-specific action plans. Used a heuristic model (product-line average price × quantity) which was practical given sklearn unavailability. Visualizations were saved. However, lacked formal train/test split and explicit validation protocol. No staged review workflow visible.

multi.brief_rationale: Multi-agent run demonstrated explicit multi-step workflow with Planner→DataScientist→Reviewer→ModelSelector→MLAnalyst phases. Included proper train/test split (80/20), RandomForest model with metrics (MAE $275.38, R² 0.9192), K-Means clustering on high-error subset, and 5 saved visualizations. Reviewer provided QA gates with CODE_NOT_APPROVED/CODE_APPROVED feedback cycles. All deliverables met: top-10 error rows table, 3 error clusters, and 4 action items per category. Stronger validation and leakage control.

scores.comparison_notes: Multi-agent run wins due to explicit multi-step workflow evidence aligning with complexity-bias policy: visible planning/review stages, reviewer-gated QA with approval checkpoints, proper train/test validation, and staged deliverable verification. Both runs meet the core task requirements (top-10 errors, 3+ categories, action plans), but multi-agent demonstrates stronger contract compliance through its phased approach with Reviewer feedback loops, formal ML methodology with performance metrics, and comprehensive artifact generation. Single agent was efficient but lacked the staged validation evidence preferred for contract-heavy analytical tasks.
